caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: The supplied 5-digit role mix is a proxy and its 62.15% residual combines physical lab, quality and administrative work.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Fractions are task-replaceability judgments, not observed headcount reductions or adoption forecasts.
inputs.pi_dist: No observed contract-type mix for US $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 providers was reopened.
inputs.pi_dist: Global preclinical-CRO pricing categories are a disclosed proxy for the target segment.
inputs.pi_moat: Public operator and market evidence do not measure target-specific customer concentration, assay differentiation or sponsor switching costs.
inputs.t50_years: BCG is a 2022 strategic publication and FDA submission experience is not a prevalence survey of small CROs.
inputs.t50_years: Effective adoption is stricter than use of an AI tool or an AI-supported submission.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No direct NAICS 541714 adoption census was found; this is not an observed firm-share statistic.
inputs.historical_analogs: This qualitative analogue is not a measured adoption curve for contract research laboratories.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: No direct owner-age statistic for EBITDA-positive NAICS 541714 targets was reopened.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Absence of reopened documentation is not evidence that no succession issue exists.
inputs.active_consolidators: This is not a census of NAICS 541714 buyers and broader biopharma-services deal activity includes clinical, CDMO and other adjacent businesses.
inputs.buy_mult: KPMG's disclosed examples are broader biopharma services and are not size-matched preclinical-CRO buy comps.
inputs.buy_mult: No direct $1-10M EBITDA NAICS 541714 transaction series was reopened.
inputs.exit_mult: The cited 11.5x example is not a preclinical-CRO, small-platform or NAICS 541714 exit comparable.
inputs.exit_mult: No direct small-lab exit series was reopened.
inputs.pricing_structure: The source describes the global preclinical-CRO market rather than the actual contract mix of small US targets.

q.rationale: The existing v3 distribution-retention × moat-retention judgment is reused as a bounded Stage 1 commercial-retention proxy.

leaves.rationale: Predeclared bounded demand/operator row for the inherited v3 terminal class; unknown classes remain missing.

e.rationale: Global eligible-union share; heterogeneous records use the predeclared wider/lower class-count row.

n.detail: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $506,178 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541714); class <5: 2,793 firms, avg revenue/firm $684,545 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $107,131 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 701 firms, avg revenue/firm $2,517,970 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $394,062 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 476 firms, avg revenue/firm $5,808,891 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $909,091 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 599 firms, avg revenue/firm $18,599,482 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $2,910,819 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 242 firms, avg revenue/firm $94,118,694 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $14,729,576 -> out of $1-10M band; class 500+: 126 firms, avg revenue/firm $297,425,317 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $46,547,062 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 599

s5.rationale: Fixed transform of the disclosed v3 owner-age range into five-year qualifying control-transfer propensity.

a.rationale: AI exposure is substantial in scientific drafting, analysis, bioinformatics and operating paperwork, but most laboratory and in-vivo delivery is physical. Fractions measure task share, not layoffs or adoption.

l.detail: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541714 ($65,745,913,502) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541714 ($77,835,466,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

rho.rationale: Fixed outcome-blind transform of the v3 t50 adoption estimate, widened by the declared bridge error.

source_review.summary: All deterministic mechanics pass and every registered source was accessible. The cautious kill thesis remains useful: sources support the direction of gradual AI adoption, durable physical and regulated work, mixed pricing mechanics, and broad biopharma-services deal activity. The score is nevertheless built from disclosed, low-confidence proxies for small-CRO adoption, owner age, buyer count, contract mix, and entry/exit multiples; these are publication caveats rather than material defects because the proxy limitations are explicit and the conclusion is conservative.

sources.title: Business Enterprise Research and Development (BERD) Survey 2023
sources.what_it_supports: Official context for US business R&D and the fact that BERD covers businesses performing or funding R&D, rather than a census of fee-for-service CRO EBITDA targets.

sources.title: Adopting AI in Drug Discovery
sources.what_it_supports: AI use cases in drug discovery, partnership-led adoption, and the distinction between individual applications and end-to-end AI-enabled discovery.

sources.title: FDA Proposes Framework to Advance Credibility of AI Models Used for Drug and Biological Product Submissions
sources.what_it_supports: FDA's January 2025 AI guidance context, including its experience with more than 500 drug and biological product submissions containing AI components since 2016.

sources.title: Preclinical CRO Market Size and Share Report
sources.what_it_supports: Preclinical CRO service categories, pricing-model alternatives, and the importance of outsourcing, regulatory requirements and scientific complexity to the market.

sources.title: Inotiv Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year Financial Results for Fiscal 2025
sources.what_it_supports: A public preclinical-services operator's reported discovery, translational science, safety-assessment and research-model service activity.

sources.title: Biopharma Services Industry Update H1 2024
sources.what_it_supports: Biopharma-services deal activity, financial-sponsor activity and selected disclosed transaction EBITDA multiples, with broader-market limitations.

title: Research and Development in Biotechnology (except Nanobiotechnology)
